records.ety: [L. imprecatus, p. p. of imprecari to imprecate; pref. im- in, on + precari to pray. See Pray.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Imprecated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Imprecating (?).]

defs: To call down by prayer, as something hurtful or calamitous.
quotes: Imprecate the vengeance of Heaven on the guilty empire.

defs: To invoke evil upon; to curse; to swear at.
quotes: In vain we blast the ministers of Fate, And the forlorn physicians imprecate.
